behaviors.description: Group log entries by trace ID across different microservices.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (ingestion/normalization + correlation), not just a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process fixtures from multiple services are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: no behavior text about real HTTP, subprocesses, or external systems, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the ingest + correlate modules together with mixed-service JSON fixtures where identical trace IDs appear across services; assert one correlated group per trace ID regardless of service origin.

behaviors.description: Order timeline entries by timestamp while handling up to 500ms inter-service clock skew.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk appears when parsed timestamps, service identity, and skew-adjusted ordering interact across modules. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real network/OS/subprocess in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text only specifies timestamp ordering/skew logic, not infrastructure-dependent conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + skew-adjustment + timeline builder with synthetic multi-service logs containing known ±500ms skew; assert final timeline matches expected causal order and remains stable at the skew boundary.

behaviors.description: Detect and flag latency spikes when p99 exceeds 3x baseline.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is threshold/statistical logic in anomaly detection; can be isolated in one function/object. REAL-INFRA: none required by behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require module boundary effects, real infra, or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Test anomaly detector function with deterministic latency distributions and baseline values around the 3x cutoff; assert p99 > 3x baseline is flagged and <= 3x is not.

behaviors.description: Skip malformed JSON without crashing pipeline and report skipped-entry count.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure involves interaction between parser error handling, pipeline continuation, and reporting counter. Unit parser tests alone can miss pipeline crash/count propagation bugs. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: no explicit real infrastructure dependency in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parse->process->report pipeline in-process with a mixed stream of valid and malformed lines; assert processing completes, valid entries continue downstream, and skipped count equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: Sustain burst load of 10,000 log entries/second without drops or lag.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is backpressure/queueing interaction across ingest, buffering, and processing modules under load; single-function tests are insufficient. REAL-INFRA: behavior text specifies throughput target but does not explicitly require real network/subprocess/filesystem, so an in-process load harness can be minimum. Rejected higher level: no concrete text requiring real HTTP, subprocess orchestration, or external infra; therefore System is not strictly required as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process load test wiring the full local pipeline, inject 10,000 entries/sec for a sustained window, and assert total in == total processed plus acceptable in-flight bound, with end-to-end lag staying within configured limit.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If production ingestion is socket/file-watcher based, add a System perf test as a higher-level guard.
